# term

```go
import "github.com/pkg/term"
```

Package term manages POSIX terminals. As POSIX terminals are connected to, or emulate, a UART, this package also provides control over the various UART and serial line parameters.

## Constants

<a name="NONE"></a>

```go
const (
    NONE     = iota // flow control off
    XONXOFF         // software flow control
    HARDWARE        // hardware flow control
)
```

<a name="SetAttr"></a>

## func [SetAttr](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/term_posix.go#L35>)

```go
func SetAttr(modifier func(*unix.Termios) uintptr) func(*Term) error
```

SetAttr returns an option function which will apply the provided modifier to a unix.Termios before using that unix.Termios to set the state of the Term. This allows a developer to manually set attributes for the terminal. Here's an example case to set a terminal into raw mode, but then re\-enable the 'opost' attribute:

```
func EnableOutputPostprocess(a *unix.Termios) uintptr {
    a.Oflag |= unix.OPOST
    return termios.TCSANOW
}

func init() {
    t, _ = term.Open("/dev/tty")
    t.SetRaw()
    t.SetOption(term.SetAttr(EnableOutputPostprocess))
}
```

<a name="Term"></a>

## type [Term](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/term_posix.go#L13-L17>)

Term represents an asynchronous communications port.

```go
type Term struct {
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Open"></a>

### func [Open](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/term_open_posix.go#L13>)

```go
func Open(name string, options ...func(*Term) error) (*Term, error)
```

Open opens an asynchronous communications port.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Open a terminal in raw mode at 19200 baud.

```go
Open("/dev/ttyUSB0", Speed(19200), RawMode)
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Term.Read"></a>

### func \(\*Term\) [Read](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/term.go#L25>)

```go
func (t *Term) Read(b []byte) (int, error)
```

Read reads up to len\(b\) bytes from the terminal. It returns the number of bytes read and an error, if any. EOF is signaled by a zero count with err set to io.EOF.

<a name="Term.Restore"></a>

### func \(\*Term\) [Restore](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/term_open_posix.go#L44>)

```go
func (t *Term) Restore() error
```

Restore restores the state of the terminal captured at the point that the terminal was originally opened.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Restore the terminal state

```go
t, _ := Open("/dev/tty")
// mutate terminal state
t.Restore()
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Term.SendBreak"></a>

### func \(\*Term\) [SendBreak](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/term_posix.go#L195>)

```go
func (t *Term) SendBreak() error
```

SendBreak sends a break signal.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Send Break to the remote DTE.

```go
t, _ := Open("/dev/ttyUSB0")
for {
 time.Sleep(3 * time.Second)
 log.Println("Break...")
 t.SendBreak()
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Term.SetCbreak"></a>

### func \(\*Term\) [SetCbreak](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/term_posix.go#L47>)

```go
func (t *Term) SetCbreak() error
```

SetCbreak sets cbreak mode.

<a name="Term.SetDTR"></a>

### func \(\*Term\) [SetDTR](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/term_posix.go#L206>)

```go
func (t *Term) SetDTR(v bool) error
```

SetDTR sets the DTR \(data terminal ready\) signal.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

Reset an Arduino by toggling the DTR signal.

```go
t, _ := Open("/dev/USB0")
t.SetDTR(false) // toggle DTR low
time.Sleep(250 * time.Millisecond)
t.SetDTR(true) // raise DTR, resets Ardunio
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Term.SetSpeed"></a>

### func \(\*Term\) [SetSpeed](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/term_posix.go#L84>)

```go
func (t *Term) SetSpeed(baud int) error
```

<a name="Term.Write"></a>

### func \(\*Term\) [Write](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/term.go#L55>)

```go
func (t *Term) Write(b []byte) (int, error)
```

Write writes len\(b\) bytes to the terminal. It returns the number of bytes written and an error, if any. Write returns a non\-nil error when n \!= len\(b\).

# termios

```go
import "github.com/pkg/term/termios"
```

Package termios implements the low level termios\(3\) terminal line discipline facilities.

For a higher level interface please use the github.com/pkg/term package.

## Constants

<a name="IXON"></a>

```go
const (
    IXON       = 0x00000200
    IXOFF      = 0x00000400
    IXANY      = 0x00000800
    CCTS_OFLOW = 0x00010000
    CRTS_IFLOW = 0x00020000
    CRTSCTS    = CCTS_OFLOW | CRTS_IFLOW
)
```

<a name="TCIFLUSH"></a>

```go
const (
    TCIFLUSH  = 0
    TCOFLUSH  = 1
    TCIOFLUSH = 2

    TCSANOW   = 0
    TCSADRAIN = 1
    TCSAFLUSH = 2
)
```

## func [Pty](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/termios/pty.go#L22>)

```go
func Pty() (*os.File, *os.File, error)
```

Pty returns a UNIX 98 pseudoterminal device. Pty returns a pair of fds representing the master and slave pair.

<a name="Tcdrain"></a>

## func [Tcdrain](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/termios/termios_bsd.go#L52>)

```go
func Tcdrain(fd uintptr) error
```

Tcdrain waits until all output written to the terminal referenced by fd has been transmitted to the terminal.

<a name="Tcflush"></a>

## func [Tcflush](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/termios/termios_bsd.go#L57>)

```go
func Tcflush(fd, which uintptr) error
```

Tcflush discards data written to the object referred to by fd but not transmitted, or data received but not read, depending on the value of which.

<a name="Tcgetattr"></a>

## func [Tcgetattr](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/termios/termios_bsd.go#L21>)

```go
func Tcgetattr(fd uintptr) (*unix.Termios, error)
```

Tcgetattr gets the current serial port settings.

<a name="Tcsendbreak"></a>

## func [Tcsendbreak](<https://github.com/pkg/term/blob/master/termios/termios_bsd.go#L43>)

```go
func Tcsendbreak(fd uintptr, duration int) error
```

Tcsendbreak function transmits a continuous stream of zero\-valued bits for four\-tenths of a second to the terminal referenced by fildes. The duration parameter is ignored in this implementation.

Tcsetattr sets the current serial port settings.
